statement.markdown: Given two integers n and k, calculate the binomial coefficient C(n, k) (also known as n choose k) in a way that minimizes overflows.
statement.inputFormat: Input will consist of two integers n and k separated by a space.
statement.outputFormat: Output the result of C(n, k) as an integer.
statement.notes: You may not use the built-in math library to calculate the binomial coefficient.

hints.text: Pay attention to the mathematical formula of the binomial coefficient and how to accurately calculate it.

editorial.title: Accurate Binomial Coefficient Calculation
editorial.intuition: Mathematically, C(n, k) is the ratio of n!/(k!(n-k)!). We can calculate this using the factorial formula.
editorial.bruteForce: Directly calculate the factorials and then divide them.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a loop to calculate the factorials.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a loop to calculate the factorials and then divide them, minimizing overflow.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The formula C(n, k) = n!/(k!(n-k)!) is mathematically correct.
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution with known inputs to ensure accuracy.
